{"id":"project-LICHEN-58l3","title":"protocol.py: UnicodeDecodeError not wrapped as ProtocolError","description":"File: python/src/lichen/sim/protocol.py
Lines: 146, 156, 688

decode_register and decode_err use .decode('utf-8') which can raise UnicodeDecodeError for invalid UTF-8 sequences:

    sim_id = data[offset : offset + sim_id_len].decode('utf-8')
    node_id = data[offset : offset + node_id_len].decode('utf-8')
    msg = data[2 : 2 + msg_len].decode('utf-8')

Callers (e.g., node_server.py line 269) only catch ProtocolError, not UnicodeDecodeError. A malicious client sending invalid UTF-8 causes an unhandled exception that bubbles up to the generic exception handler rather than returning a proper error response.

Fix: Wrap .decode() calls in try/except UnicodeDecodeError and re-raise as ProtocolError.

Impact: Malformed messages cause unhandled exceptions instead of proper error responses.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:27:52.471778Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:27:52.471809Z"}
